proposals.hypothesis: Reimagine is aspirational fluff with 0 conversion signal. Spec-first mirrors sdd's converting pattern (Move Up the Stack to SDD, Spec-Driven Development both convert). Orchestration searchers who think spec-first are the same high-intent buyers.

34 clicks, 0 conversions — keyword stuffing fails. New headline speaks directly to git worktree searchers: Intent runs agents inside worktrees, so this frames it as the next step in their workflow. Action verb + specific mechanism.

agentic ide search term converts (~100% CR on 1 click). Automated Agent Workflows is generic. Direct name-match to the converting search term. Ade already converts at 10.89% — this headline targets the highest-intent searcher in the group.
proposals.failure_reason: DKI headline {KeyWord:Agentic Dev Environment} is 33 chars, exceeds tool maxLength:30 schema — cannot reproduce in new RSA without losing DKI

proposals.original: Run multiple AI coding agents in parallel without conflicts.
proposals.new_text: Ralph loops run faster in Intent. Visual diffs, parallel worktrees, no conflicts.
proposals.hypothesis: 167 clicks, 0 conv. This exact text converts at 10% in conductor but fails for ralph — audience mismatch. Ralph searchers need transition value from their existing workflow, not a generic parallel agents pitch. Ralph-specific framing tests whether named continuity converts.

proposals.original: Intent's Coordinator breaks down work, specialist agents execute everything.
proposals.new_text: Your Ralph prompts. Intent's workspace. Add parallel worktrees and visual diffs.
proposals.hypothesis: 165 clicks, 0 conv. Abstract coordinator language doesn't convert. Concrete tool transfer (your prompts → our workspace) mirrors the Claude Code in One Workspace headline pattern (7 conv). Ownership framing reduces switching friction.

proposals.original: Orchestrate multiple AI agents with integrated terminal, browser & IDE context
proposals.new_text: Spec-to-PR in one workspace. Agents implement in parallel, you review the diff.
proposals.hypothesis: 245 clicks, 0 conv. Capability list (terminal, browser, IDE) doesn't convert for sdd intent. Concrete workflow outcome (spec → PR, you review the diff) mirrors the converting headline pattern and puts the buyer in control.

proposals.original: A unified workspace for SDD. Integrated terminal, browser, and agents for faster coding
proposals.new_text: Write a spec, agents handle the rest. Terminal, diffs, and preview built in.
proposals.hypothesis: 132 clicks, 0 conv. Unified workspace is generic. Write a spec mirrors claude_code_mac's 8% CR description pattern. Tooling callout (terminal, diffs, preview) provides concrete grounding without feature-list bloat.
